name: tmux Workstation Builder
description: Composes a tmux workspace (layout + panes) into a ready-to-paste one-liner. Static site, no backend. State is set via URL query params or the generate.sh bash script, so an agent composes a workspace and hands the result to the user.

skills.name: generate_command
skills.description: Generate a tmux one-liner from layout + panes. RECOMMENDED path: fetch and run /generate.sh (pure bash, zero deps) — outputs the command to stdout. No browser needed.

flags.--path: working dir (default: . — current dir; NEVER use a placeholder path)
flags.--detached: 1|0 — 1 adds -d (background, for agents); 0 drops user in (default: 0)
flags.--exec: flag (no value) — pass commands as pane processes (reliable for agents; no timing race)

input.agent_mode: For agents launching in the background: add --detached 1 --exec. Then tell the user: tmux attach -t NAME
input.post_launch_verify: After launching detached, verify: (1) tmux list-panes -t NAME | wc -l must equal your pane count, (2) tmux list-panes -t NAME -F '#{pane_index}: #{pane_current_command}' must show each pane running the expected app. A missing pane = a failed command.

skills.name: compose_workspace
skills.description: Compose a tmux workspace as a URL (for humans or JS-capable agents). Returns a shareable URL.

input.instructions: Build a URL with query params: name, path, layout, detached, exec, panes. Open it to read the generated command from element #output (requires JS).

skills.name: explore_options
skills.description: Discover available layouts, commands, and templates.

input.instructions: Fetch /options.json for the full structured catalogue (includes generate.sh flags and examples), or /llms.txt for the task-oriented agent manual.
